hypothesis: A nonzero USD headline CPI surprise continues in the implied USD direction after the mandatory two- or three-hour blackout because institutional repricing is not instantaneous.

engine_contract.event: USD high-impact CPI m/m rows with numeric actual and forecast
engine_contract.timestamp_transform: validated local timestamp +1 day -7 hours to UTC
engine_contract.surprise: actual percentage points minus forecast percentage points
engine_contract.direction: Positive surprise: short EURUSD and GBPUSD, long USDJPY; negative surprise reverses all three legs.
engine_contract.entry: First common M5 open at or after the corrected event time plus the fixed blackout.
engine_contract.sizing: Equal initial ATR risk across the three legs; package R is their arithmetic mean.
engine_contract.exit: Independent fixed ATR stop or target, otherwise time exit after the fixed number of available M5 bars.
engine_contract.rollover_stress: For holds of eight hours or longer add one current worst-side swap allowance: EURUSD 0.0001103, GBPUSD 0.0000577, USDJPY 0.02241.

selection_contract.winner_rule: Maximize min(development PF, validation PF), then development trades.
selection_contract.holdout_open_rule: Open 2024-2025 only for the one locked global preholdout winner.
selection_contract.holdout_pass_rule: At least 15 trades, pooled PF >= 1.10, positive pooled net R, and positive net R in both 2024 and 2025.

label: RESEARCH_ONLY_NO_GO
